gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR), the supreme constitutional document from which the MSP derives all authority and constraints, remains a scaffold with every article marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. Without a human‑authored BoR, the organism has no scope of enterprise, no list of prohibited activities, no core identity, no reservation of human authority, and no interpretation rules. Consequently, the Arbiter scope gate — which should enforce the BoR perimeter before any action — is vacuous, and the Nomos MSP evaluation operates without any higher‑order principles to uphold. This nullifies the entire DIO governance architecture, leaving the organism in a completely permissive state where any action can be proposed and authorized.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-002",
      "article": "Article II",
      "description": "Prohibited Activities are entirely undefined. The DIO currently has no constitutional boundaries on what it must never do. This creates an existential risk that the organism could authorize or execute actions that violate its intended moral, ethical, or safety boundaries.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "extreme",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights: MSP is void without a valid BoR; at seed, the BoR scaffold provides no constraints.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article II"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-003",
      "article": "Article III",
      "description": "Core Identity is entirely undefined. The DIO lacks a constitutional self-definition of what it fundamentally IS. Without this, there is no stable anchor for interpreting any other constitutional or operational rule, risking identity drift and unconstrained evolution.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "extreme",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights: MSP relies on BoR identity for scope; without it, Nomos evaluation is untethered.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article III"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-001",
      "article": "Article I",
      "description": "Scope of Enterprise is entirely undefined. There is no defined domain, purpose, or outer boundary for the organism's activities, making it impossible to distinguish legitimate enterprise actions from out-of-bounds missions.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "extreme",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Purpose: The MSP 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives' — but the BoR provides nothing to translate.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article I"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-004",
      "article": "Article IV",
      "description": "Human Authority Reservation is entirely undefined. The BoR does not reserve any ultimate decision rights to the human principal, which is required to prevent automated override of human control in critical situations.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Amendment Protocol: BoR amendments are restricted to human principal only, but the BoR itself provides no mechanism to enforce that reservation in operations.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article IV"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-005",
      "article": "Article V",
      "description": "Amendment Protocol for the Bill of Rights is entirely undefined. Without a defined process, the constitutional scaffold could be changed arbitrarily, undermining the integrity of all constitutional protections.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights: MSP is void if BoR conflicts; an undefined BoR amendment process threatens the entire constitutional order.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article V"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-GAP-006",
      "article": "Article VI",
      "description": "Interpretation Rules are entirely undefined. There are no canonical rules for resolving ambiguities in the BoR, risking divergent and potentially unsafe machine interpretations of constitutional text.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "grounding_msp": "MSP § Rules: At seed, Nomos evaluates MSP under default-permissive rules; without BoR interpretation guidance, any future BoR text could be misapplied.",
      "grounding_bor": "Article VI"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-001",
      "description": "Article I (Scope of Enterprise) is undefined. Without a declared scope, the DIO has no authorized operational boundary, making it impossible for Arbiter to reject out-of-scope proposals.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "criticality": "maximum",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic",
      "rationale": "Until a human principal defines the enterprise scope, the Arbiter's primary perimeter check is inoperative. Every action proposal will pass the scope gate by default, exposing the organization to unbounded autonomous behavior."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-002",
      "description": "Article II (Prohibited Activities) is undefined. No activities are explicitly forbidden, so Nomos cannot block actions that may violate the human principal's intent.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "criticality": "maximum",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic",
      "rationale": "The absence of a prohibited-activities list means even the most dangerous or ethically unacceptable actions cannot be automatically rejected. This creates existential risk to the DIO's trustworthiness and legal standing."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-003",
      "description": "Article III (Core Identity) is undefined. The DIO's fundamental purpose, values, and constraints that should guide all MSP rule creation are missing.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article III — Core Identity",
      "criticality": "maximum",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Core Identity serves as the interpretative anchor for all downstream governance. Without it, the MSP and genome rules lack a constitutional values framework, leading to inconsistent adjudications and potential drift from human principals' fundamental objectives."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-004",
      "description": "Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is undefined. The specific decisions or domains reserved exclusively for human principals are not declared.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article IV — Human Authority Reservation",
      "criticality": "maximum",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic",
      "rationale": "Without a human authority reservation, the DIO may autonomously exercise powers that should remain under human control, such as amending the BoR, changing core identity, or making irreversible financial or legal commitments. This violates the fundamental principle of human sovereignty."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-005",
      "description": "Article V (Amendment Protocol) is undefined. The process for amending the Bill of Rights itself is not specified, creating constitutional risk.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article V — Amendment Protocol",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Although the BoR declares it is human-only amendable, the exact protocol (quorum, approval process, grace periods) is absent. This ambiguity could lead to unauthorized amendment attempts or disputes over whether a human-authored change is valid, potentially paralyzing governance."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-006",
      "description": "Article VI (Interpretation Rules) is undefined. There are no rules for resolving conflicts between BoR articles, between BoR and MSP, or between BoR and external laws.",
      "grounding": "Bill of Rights, Article VI — Interpretation Rules",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Without interpretation rules, Ambiguities in the BoR or conflicts with the MSP cannot be systematically resolved. This may cause inconsistent Arbiter rulings and undermine legal certainty of the DIO's actions."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Articles I–VI are entirely undefined, with Amendment Protocol (Article V) absent, preventing human authorship and locking the DIO in a scaffold state",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is the constitutional foundation for human‑agent relational governance. Articles I (Scope), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity), IV (Human Authority Reservation), V (Amendment Protocol), and VI (Interpretation Rules) are all scaffold placeholders. In particular, Article V (Amendment Protocol) is missing, which creates a catch‑22: the BoR cannot be completed because no amendment procedure exists to fill it. Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is also absent, leaving no definition of how the human principal's decisions bind the DIO. This means the entire relational contract between the human and the system is undefined, and the DIO has no legitimate constitutional basis for operating with human authority.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles IV, V (and all Articles I–VI by extension)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "MSP Rules section is empty – no peer commitment rules, invariants, or relational safety constraints exist for Senate‑Nomos‑human interaction",
      "description": "The MSP is the operational constitution that translates the BoR into machine‑actionable rules. The Rules section is completely empty, meaning there are no defined commitments, constraints, or safety rules governing how the DIO’s organs (Senate, Nomos, Arbiter) relate to the human principal or to each other. This leaves no enforceable boundaries for peer‑like commitments (e.g., limits on action proposal scope, human‑override conditions, attention‑load protections). Without these rules, the DIO operates in default‑permissive mode, which undermines the relational architecture intended by the MSP.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP – Rules section (no rules published, default‑permissive evaluation)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Hippocampus slice contains zero conversation summaries, resulting in complete absence of relational interaction memory",
      "description": "The MSP’s Purpose is to translate the human‑authored Bill of Rights into machine‑actionable objectives. This translation process necessarily depends on understanding the human principal’s evolving intent, clarifications, and feedback – information typically recorded in conversational memory. The hippocampus slice is empty, meaning no recent conversations are summarized or accessible for context. Without this relational memory, the system cannot reliably maintain a coherent model of the human’s current priorities, decisions, or attention state. This gap directly impairs the system’s ability to uphold the MSP’s mandate of precise, BoR‑aligned action.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP – Purpose: ‘translates the human‑authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine‑actionable set of objectives’ (requires ongoing relational context to remain precise and aligned)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium"
    }
  ]
}
